item.title: Small Groups
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to Small Groups Ministry!

The Children’s Ministry helps children grow and develop into spiritual teens, young adults, and ultimately spiritual men/women of God, who will make a lasting impact for the Kingdom of Christ. Through the various children’s ministries here at Shiloh, we believe that a firm spiritual foundation can be made that will anchor our children forever. Children's Church meets 2nd, 3rd, & 4th Sunday. Nursery meets every Sunday.

We have a vibrant curriculum that children love and learn from, as well as family resources designed to meet the needs of today’s families. Children are encouraged to open their hearts to God through worship and study in a safe and loving environment.
Our Nursery- Kinder Director, Monique Rios, and our Children’s Director, Juli Godsil, are both available to help you in the most important task any parent can have – helping your child develop a relationship with God!

Heaven makes space for every child and so do we.

For some kids, we have the joy of working a little harder to make sure they are welcomed well. We call this our Breakthrough ministry and we seek to support neuro-diverse and special needs children. Parents and staff collaborate to find the best fit for the child. We have three different approaches:
Early Years (Ages 0-6): Inclusion model
Elementary (1st - 6th Grade): Inclusion model
Breakthrough (1st Grade - 18 years old): Sensory sensitive classroom

We believe our church is richer for these little ones and we seek to partner with Gods’ dreams and purposes for them. For more information and to notify us that your child will be joining us, please email: example@cmsmasters.com

Meeting Times

Tuesdays, 12:30pm - 3:00pm
Thursdays, 9:00am - 11:00am

Schedule

10:00 AM - Welcome kids and playtime
10:20 AM - Worship
10:35 AM - Large group lesson
11:00 AM - Age group split off
11:05 AM - Crafts and Mini Lesson
11:45 AM - Closing time

Location

Lake Building 20 Lake Blvd.Redding, CA 96003

item.title: Life Groups
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to Life Groups Ministry!

The Children’s Ministry helps children grow and develop into spiritual teens, young adults, and ultimately spiritual men/women of God, who will make a lasting impact for the Kingdom of Christ. Through the various children’s ministries here at Shiloh, we believe that a firm spiritual foundation can be made that will anchor our children forever. Children's Church meets 2nd, 3rd, & 4th Sunday. Nursery meets every Sunday.

We have a vibrant curriculum that children love and learn from, as well as family resources designed to meet the needs of today’s families. Children are encouraged to open their hearts to God through worship and study in a safe and loving environment.
Our Nursery- Kinder Director, Monique Rios, and our Children’s Director, Juli Godsil, are both available to help you in the most important task any parent can have – helping your child develop a relationship with God!

Heaven makes space for every child and so do we.

For some kids, we have the joy of working a little harder to make sure they are welcomed well. We call this our Breakthrough ministry and we seek to support neuro-diverse and special needs children. Parents and staff collaborate to find the best fit for the child. We have three different approaches:
Early Years (Ages 0-6): Inclusion model
Elementary (1st - 6th Grade): Inclusion model
Breakthrough (1st Grade - 18 years old): Sensory sensitive classroom

We believe our church is richer for these little ones and we seek to partner with Gods’ dreams and purposes for them. For more information and to notify us that your child will be joining us, please email: example@cmsmasters.com

Meeting Times

Tuesdays, 12:30pm - 3:00pm
Thursdays, 9:00am - 11:00am

Schedule

10:00 AM - Welcome kids and playtime
10:20 AM - Worship
10:35 AM - Large group lesson
11:00 AM - Age group split off
11:05 AM - Crafts and Mini Lesson
11:45 AM - Closing time

Location

Lake Building 20 Lake Blvd.Redding, CA 96003

item.title: Children’s Ministry
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to Children’s Ministry!

The Children’s Ministry helps children grow and develop into spiritual teens, young adults, and ultimately spiritual men/women of God, who will make a lasting impact for the Kingdom of Christ. Through the various children’s ministries here at Shiloh, we believe that a firm spiritual foundation can be made that will anchor our children forever. Children's Church meets 2nd, 3rd, & 4th Sunday. Nursery meets every Sunday.

We have a vibrant curriculum that children love and learn from, as well as family resources designed to meet the needs of today’s families. Children are encouraged to open their hearts to God through worship and study in a safe and loving environment.
Our Nursery- Kinder Director, Monique Rios, and our Children’s Director, Juli Godsil, are both available to help you in the most important task any parent can have – helping your child develop a relationship with God!

Heaven makes space for every child and so do we.

For some kids, we have the joy of working a little harder to make sure they are welcomed well. We call this our Breakthrough ministry and we seek to support neuro-diverse and special needs children. Parents and staff collaborate to find the best fit for the child. We have three different approaches:
Early Years (Ages 0-6): Inclusion model
Elementary (1st - 6th Grade): Inclusion model
Breakthrough (1st Grade - 18 years old): Sensory sensitive classroom

We believe our church is richer for these little ones and we seek to partner with Gods’ dreams and purposes for them. For more information and to notify us that your child will be joining us, please email: example@cmsmasters.com

Meeting Times

Tuesdays, 12:30pm - 3:00pm
Thursdays, 9:00am - 11:00am

10:00 AM - Welcome kids and playtime

10:35 AM - Large group lesson
11:00 AM - Age group split off
11:05 AM - Crafts and Mini Lesson
11:45 AM - Closing time

Location

Lake Building 20 Lake Blvd.Redding, CA 96003

item.title: Outreach & Missions
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to Outreach & Missions Ministry!

The Children’s Ministry helps children grow and develop into spiritual teens, young adults, and ultimately spiritual men/women of God, who will make a lasting impact for the Kingdom of Christ. Through the various children’s ministries here at Shiloh, we believe that a firm spiritual foundation can be made that will anchor our children forever. Children's Church meets 2nd, 3rd, & 4th Sunday. Nursery meets every Sunday.

We have a vibrant curriculum that children love and learn from, as well as family resources designed to meet the needs of today’s families. Children are encouraged to open their hearts to God through worship and study in a safe and loving environment.
Our Nursery- Kinder Director, Monique Rios, and our Children’s Director, Juli Godsil, are both available to help you in the most important task any parent can have – helping your child develop a relationship with God!

Heaven makes space for every child and so do we.

For some kids, we have the joy of working a little harder to make sure they are welcomed well. We call this our Breakthrough ministry and we seek to support neuro-diverse and special needs children. Parents and staff collaborate to find the best fit for the child. We have three different approaches:
Early Years (Ages 0-6): Inclusion model
Elementary (1st - 6th Grade): Inclusion model
Breakthrough (1st Grade - 18 years old): Sensory sensitive classroom

We believe our church is richer for these little ones and we seek to partner with Gods’ dreams and purposes for them. For more information and to notify us that your child will be joining us, please email: example@cmsmasters.com

Meeting Times

Tuesdays, 12:30pm - 3:00pm
Thursdays, 9:00am - 11:00am

Schedule

10:00 AM - Welcome kids and playtime
10:20 AM - Worship
10:35 AM - Large group lesson
11:00 AM - Age group split off
11:05 AM - Crafts and Mini Lesson
11:45 AM - Closing time

Location

Lake Building 20 Lake Blvd.Redding, CA 96003

item.title: Mike Bennett
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy getting to know my patients and building meaningful relationships. I understand that each person is unique and that they have diverse cultural backgrounds, so I strive to actively listen and empower them to be an advocate for the health and wellness goals that are important to them. I’m dedicated to providing compassionate and individualized care by providing patient education and practicing evidence-based medicine. I’m passionate about practicing preventive medicine and helping my patients manage acute and chronic health conditions, mental health, and overall well-being.

I find joy by doing almost any outdoor activity, including hiking, running, and exploring with our furbabies. I enjoy spending time with family and friends. I also strive to live mindfully and intentionally by creating uplifting routines and relaxing rituals.

item.title: Sienna Wolfe
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded: It is a paradisematic country, in which roasted parts of sentences fly into your mouth. Even the all-powerful Pointing has no control about the blind texts it is an almost unorthographic life One day however a small line of blind text by the name of Lorem Ipsum decided to leave for the far World of Grammar. The Big Oxmox advised her not to do so, because there were thousands of bad Commas, wild Question Marks and devious Semikoli, but the Little Blind Text didn’t listen. She packed her seven versalia, put her initial into the belt and made herself on the way. item.title: Samantha Wiley
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy getting to know my patients and building meaningful relationships. I understand that each person is unique and that they have diverse cultural backgrounds, so I strive to actively listen and empower them to be an advocate for the health and wellness goals that are important to them. I’m dedicated to providing compassionate and individualized care by providing patient education and practicing evidence-based medicine. I’m passionate about practicing preventive medicine and helping my patients manage acute and chronic health conditions, mental health.

I find joy by doing almost any outdoor activity, including hiking, running, and exploring with our furbabies. I enjoy spending time with family and friends. I also strive to live mindfully and intentionally.

item.title: Help Families Today with Critical Relief
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
About the Campaign

In Mozambique, leaders face extreme challenges. Living in poverty, most leaders in the Brethren in Christ Church (BIC Church) in Mozambique have limited access to education. And their work is formidable: Together, the five BIC bishops lead over 100 congregations spanning five languages. Spread over hundreds of miles of rough terrain, the churches are difficult to reach, and the bishops do not have funding to travel the long distances. The churches and leaders also lack computers, often electricity, and administrative tools and training, making communication complicated. Additionally, bishops receive little money from their churches to cover ministry expenses.

One bishop lamented practical needs surrounding church leadership, “Of the fifty churches I oversee, forty-five are infants, but I have no money to visit them.”

The church is young and struggling to develop in a volatile postwar context. Passionate and willing pastors are available and hungry to be trained; however, many challenges exist in reaching these leaders with theological and ministry training. Without trained leaders, the church is lacking the foundation to grow and thrive.

Join us in providing leadership and theological train ing to the five

bishops leading the church in Mozambique. Your gift will also help provide a small stipend to sustain their ministry until they can establish financial support from among their churches.
Training seminars are held for the bishops four times per year for three days each. During these seminars, bishops learn leadership skills and theology to equip them in facilitating the work of the BIC Church. Equipped by these seminars, bishops can then encourage pastors in sound biblical teaching and missions outreach; the spiritual and leadership growth of the bishops directly impacts the health of the entire church.

Pastors and church members are eager to receive biblical teaching from their bishops, and visits the bishops make to local congregations greatly encourage local believers in their Christian walk. We anticipate the day when bishops will be regularly visiting their churches to encourage spiritual maturity.

Trained and empowered bishops will provide balanced and sustainable leadership to encourage the BIC Church in Mozambique to more fully know and follow Jesus.

By giving to the Equipping Church Leaders project, you help leaders shepherd the developing church in Mozambique.

Donation Details

From our campaigners and lawyers to local groups and supporters, we push for change on causes that matter to you, like:
  • Priests in parishes where the first collection is insufficient to meet their stipend.
  • The support of the Archbishop, Vicar General and Chancellor.
  • Priests in remote dioceses across United States.
  • Clergy Retirement Foundation (in support of sick and retired clergy).
  • Each priest in a parish draws a personal stipend from the first collection for living expenses, food and housekeeping.

Give Today

Every time you give to FindFaith Church, you’re helping fulfill the mission to reach people for Christ.
Share this Campaign:
item.excerpt:encoded: Together, let's make a meaningful impact by providing essential assistance and fostering a brighter, more secure future for families in need.

item.content:encoded:
About the Campaign

In Mozambique, leaders face extreme challenges. Living in poverty, most leaders in the Brethren in Christ Church (BIC Church) in Mozambique have limited access to education. And their work is formidable: Together, the five BIC bishops lead over 100 congregations spanning five languages. Spread over hundreds of miles of rough terrain, the churches are difficult to reach, and the bishops do not have funding to travel the long distances. The churches and leaders also lack computers, often electricity, and administrative tools and training, making communication complicated. Additionally, bishops receive little money from their churches to cover ministry expenses.

One bishop lamented practical needs surrounding church leadership, “Of the fifty churches I oversee, forty-five are infants, but I have no money to visit them.”

The church is young and struggling to develop in a volatile postwar context. Passionate and willing pastors are available and hungry to be trained; however, many challenges exist in reaching these leaders with theological and ministry training. Without trained leaders, the church is lacking the foundation to grow and thrive.

Join us in providing leadership and theological train ing to the five

bishops leading the church in Mozambique. Your gift will also help provide a small stipend to sustain their ministry until they can establish financial support from among their churches.
Training seminars are held for the bishops four times per year for three days each. During these seminars, bishops learn leadership skills and theology to equip them in facilitating the work of the BIC Church. Equipped by these seminars, bishops can then encourage pastors in sound biblical teaching and missions outreach; the spiritual and leadership growth of the bishops directly impacts the health of the entire church.

Pastors and church members are eager to receive biblical teaching from their bishops, and visits the bishops make to local congregations greatly encourage local believers in their Christian walk. We anticipate the day when bishops will be regularly visiting their churches to encourage spiritual maturity.

Trained and empowered bishops will provide balanced and sustainable leadership to encourage the BIC Church in Mozambique to more fully know and follow Jesus.

By giving to the Equipping Church Leaders project, you help leaders shepherd the developing church in Mozambique.

Donation Details

From our campaigners and lawyers to local groups and supporters, we push for change on causes that matter to you, like:
  • Priests in parishes where the first collection is insufficient to meet their stipend.
  • The support of the Archbishop, Vicar General and Chancellor.
  • Priests in remote dioceses across United States.
  • Clergy Retirement Foundation (in support of sick and retired clergy).
  • Each priest in a parish draws a personal stipend from the first collection for living expenses, food and housekeeping.

Give Today

Every time you give to FindFaith Church, you’re helping fulfill the mission to reach people for Christ.
Share this Campaign:
item.excerpt:encoded: Together, let's shape the future of church leadership through strategic training and thoughtful investment in the leaders of today and tomorrow.

item.title: Discipling Youth and Young Adults
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
About the Campaign

In Mozambique, leaders face extreme challenges. Living in poverty, most leaders in the Brethren in Christ Church (BIC Church) in Mozambique have limited access to education. And their work is formidable: Together, the five BIC bishops lead over 100 congregations spanning five languages. Spread over hundreds of miles of rough terrain, the churches are difficult to reach, and the bishops do not have funding to travel the long distances. The churches and leaders also lack computers, often electricity, and administrative tools and training, making communication complicated. Additionally, bishops receive little money from their churches to cover ministry expenses.

One bishop lamented practical needs surrounding church leadership, “Of the fifty churches I oversee, forty-five are infants, but I have no money to visit them.”

The church is young and struggling to develop in a volatile postwar context. Passionate and willing pastors are available and hungry to be trained; however, many challenges exist in reaching these leaders with theological and ministry training. Without trained leaders, the church is lacking the foundation to grow and thrive.

Join us in providing leadership and theological train ing to the five

bishops leading the church in Mozambique. Your gift will also help provide a small stipend to sustain their ministry until they can establish financial support from among their churches.
Training seminars are held for the bishops four times per year for three days each. During these seminars, bishops learn leadership skills and theology to equip them in facilitating the work of the BIC Church. Equipped by these seminars, bishops can then encourage pastors in sound biblical teaching and missions outreach; the spiritual and leadership growth of the bishops directly impacts the health of the entire church.

Pastors and church members are eager to receive biblical teaching from their bishops, and visits the bishops make to local congregations greatly encourage local believers in their Christian walk. We anticipate the day when bishops will be regularly visiting their churches to encourage spiritual maturity.

Trained and empowered bishops will provide balanced and sustainable leadership to encourage the BIC Church in Mozambique to more fully know and follow Jesus.

By giving to the Equipping Church Leaders project, you help leaders shepherd the developing church in Mozambique.

Donation Details

From our campaigners and lawyers to local groups and supporters, we push for change on causes that matter to you, like:
  • Priests in parishes where the first collection is insufficient to meet their stipend.
  • The support of the Archbishop, Vicar General and Chancellor.
  • Priests in remote dioceses across United States.
  • Clergy Retirement Foundation (in support of sick and retired clergy).
  • Each priest in a parish draws a personal stipend from the first collection for living expenses, food and housekeeping.

Give Today

Every time you give to FindFaith Church, you’re helping fulfill the mission to reach people for Christ.
Share this Campaign:
item.excerpt:encoded: Your contribution isn't just a donation; it's a powerful affirmation of the belief that every young person deserves the opportunity to explore, strengthen, and share their faith in a nurturing and supportive community.

item.title: Sustaining Church Hospitals and Schools
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
About the Campaign

In Mozambique, leaders face extreme challenges. Living in poverty, most leaders in the Brethren in Christ Church (BIC Church) in Mozambique have limited access to education. And their work is formidable: Together, the five BIC bishops lead over 100 congregations spanning five languages. Spread over hundreds of miles of rough terrain, the churches are difficult to reach, and the bishops do not have funding to travel the long distances. The churches and leaders also lack computers, often electricity, and administrative tools and training, making communication complicated. Additionally, bishops receive little money from their churches to cover ministry expenses.

One bishop lamented practical needs surrounding church leadership, “Of the fifty churches I oversee, forty-five are infants, but I have no money to visit them.”

The church is young and struggling to develop in a volatile postwar context. Passionate and willing pastors are available and hungry to be trained; however, many challenges exist in reaching these leaders with theological and ministry training. Without trained leaders, the church is lacking the foundation to grow and thrive.

Join us in providing leadership and theological train ing to the five

bishops leading the church in Mozambique. Your gift will also help provide a small stipend to sustain their ministry until they can establish financial support from among their churches.
Training seminars are held for the bishops four times per year for three days each. During these seminars, bishops learn leadership skills and theology to equip them in facilitating the work of the BIC Church. Equipped by these seminars, bishops can then encourage pastors in sound biblical teaching and missions outreach; the spiritual and leadership growth of the bishops directly impacts the health of the entire church.

Pastors and church members are eager to receive biblical teaching from their bishops, and visits the bishops make to local congregations greatly encourage local believers in their Christian walk. We anticipate the day when bishops will be regularly visiting their churches to encourage spiritual maturity.

Trained and empowered bishops will provide balanced and sustainable leadership to encourage the BIC Church in Mozambique to more fully know and follow Jesus.

By giving to the Equipping Church Leaders project, you help leaders shepherd the developing church in Mozambique.

Donation Details

From our campaigners and lawyers to local groups and supporters, we push for change on causes that matter to you, like:
  • Priests in parishes where the first collection is insufficient to meet their stipend.
  • The support of the Archbishop, Vicar General and Chancellor.
  • Priests in remote dioceses across United States.
  • Clergy Retirement Foundation (in support of sick and retired clergy).
  • Each priest in a parish draws a personal stipend from the first collection for living expenses, food and housekeeping.

Give Today

Every time you give to FindFaith Church, you’re helping fulfill the mission to reach people for Christ.
Share this Campaign:
item.excerpt:encoded: Your donation isn't just a financial contribution; it's a commitment to nurturing a community where education thrives, and healthcare is accessible to all.

item.title: University Scholarships for At-Risk Youth
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters

Join us in providing leadership and theological train ing to the five

bishops leading the church in Mozambique. Your gift will also help provide a small stipend to sustain their ministry until they can establish financial support from among their churches.
Training seminars are held for the bishops four times per year for three days each. During these seminars, bishops learn leadership skills and theology to equip them in facilitating the work of the BIC Church. Equipped by these seminars, bishops can then encourage pastors in sound biblical teaching and missions outreach; the spiritual and leadership growth of the bishops directly impacts the health of the entire church.

Pastors and church members are eager to receive biblical teaching from their bishops, and visits the bishops make to local congregations greatly encourage local believers in their Christian walk. We anticipate the day when bishops will be regularly visiting their churches to encourage spiritual maturity.

Trained and empowered bishops will provide balanced and sustainable leadership to encourage the BIC Church in Mozambique to more fully know and follow Jesus.

By giving to the Equipping Church Leaders project, you help leaders shepherd the developing church in Mozambique.

Donation Details

From our campaigners and lawyers to local groups and supporters, we push for change on causes that matter to you, like:
  • Priests in parishes where the first collection is insufficient to meet their stipend.
  • The support of the Archbishop, Vicar General and Chancellor.
  • Priests in remote dioceses across United States.
  • Clergy Retirement Foundation (in support of sick and retired clergy).
  • Each priest in a parish draws a personal stipend from the first collection for living expenses, food and housekeeping.

Give Today

Every time you give to FindFaith Church, you’re helping fulfill the mission to reach people for Christ.
Share this Campaign:
item.excerpt:encoded: Your contribution is more than a donation; it's an investment in the potential of young minds, fostering a cycle of empowerment that reverberates through families and communities.

item.title: Children’s Outreach Summer Camp
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
About the Campaign

In Mozambique, leaders face extreme challenges. Living in poverty, most leaders in the Brethren in Christ Church (BIC Church) in Mozambique have limited access to education. And their work is formidable: Together, the five BIC bishops lead over 100 congregations spanning five languages. Spread over hundreds of miles of rough terrain, the churches are difficult to reach, and the bishops do not have funding to travel the long distances. The churches and leaders also lack computers, often electricity, and administrative tools and training, making communication complicated. Additionally, bishops receive little money from their churches to cover ministry expenses.

One bishop lamented practical needs surrounding church leadership, “Of the fifty churches I oversee, forty-five are infants, but I have no money to visit them.”

The church is young and struggling to develop in a volatile postwar context. Passionate and willing pastors are available and hungry to be trained; however, many challenges exist in reaching these leaders with theological and ministry training. Without trained leaders, the church is lacking the foundation to grow and thrive.

Join us in providing leadership and theological train ing to the five

bishops leading the church in Mozambique. Your gift will also help provide a small stipend to sustain their ministry until they can establish financial support from among their churches.
Training seminars are held for the bishops four times per year for three days each. During these seminars, bishops learn leadership skills and theology to equip them in facilitating the work of the BIC Church. Equipped by these seminars, bishops can then encourage pastors in sound biblical teaching and missions outreach; the spiritual and leadership growth of the bishops directly impacts the health of the entire church.

Pastors and church members are eager to receive biblical teaching from their bishops, and visits the bishops make to local congregations greatly encourage local believers in their Christian walk. We anticipate the day when bishops will be regularly visiting their churches to encourage spiritual maturity.

Trained and empowered bishops will provide balanced and sustainable leadership to encourage the BIC Church in Mozambique to more fully know and follow Jesus.

By giving to the Equipping Church Leaders project, you help leaders shepherd the developing church in Mozambique.

Donation Details

From our campaigners and lawyers to local groups and supporters, we push for change on causes that matter to you, like:
  • Priests in parishes where the first collection is insufficient to meet their stipend.
  • The support of the Archbishop, Vicar General and Chancellor.
  • Priests in remote dioceses across United States.
  • Clergy Retirement Foundation (in support of sick and retired clergy).
  • Each priest in a parish draws a personal stipend from the first collection for living expenses, food and housekeeping.

Give Today

Every time you give to FindFaith Church, you’re helping fulfill the mission to reach people for Christ.
Share this Campaign:
item.excerpt:encoded: It's a chance to make a meaningful impact, ensuring that the SummerJoy experience is accessible to every child, regardless of their background or financial circumstances.

item.title: Worship Ministry
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to Worship Ministry!

The Children’s Ministry helps children grow and develop into spiritual teens, young adults, and ultimately spiritual men/women of God, who will make a lasting impact for the Kingdom of Christ. Through the various children’s ministries here at Shiloh, we believe that a firm spiritual foundation can be made that will anchor our children forever. Children's Church meets 2nd, 3rd, & 4th Sunday. Nursery meets every Sunday.

We have a vibrant curriculum that children love and learn from, as well as family resources designed to meet the needs of today’s families. Children are encouraged to open their hearts to God through worship and study in a safe and loving environment.
Our Nursery- Kinder Director, Monique Rios, and our Children’s Director, Juli Godsil, are both available to help you in the most important task any parent can have – helping your child develop a relationship with God!

Heaven makes space for every child and so do we.

For some kids, we have the joy of working a little harder to make sure they are welcomed well. We call this our Breakthrough ministry and we seek to support neuro-diverse and special needs children. Parents and staff collaborate to find the best fit for the child. We have three different approaches:
Early Years (Ages 0-6): Inclusion model
Elementary (1st - 6th Grade): Inclusion model
Breakthrough (1st Grade - 18 years old): Sensory sensitive classroom

We believe our church is richer for these little ones and we seek to partner with Gods’ dreams and purposes for them. For more information and to notify us that your child will be joining us, please email: example@cmsmasters.com

Meeting Times

Tuesdays, 12:30pm - 3:00pm
Thursdays, 9:00am - 11:00am

Schedule

10:00 AM - Welcome kids and playtime
10:20 AM - Worship
10:35 AM - Large group lesson
11:00 AM - Age group split off
11:05 AM - Crafts and Mini Lesson
11:45 AM - Closing time

Location

Lake Building 20 Lake Blvd.Redding, CA 96003

item.title: About Us
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Home│No breadcrumbs

A Diverse and Inclusive Community

Our curriculum appeals to student diversity and interest, and is rigorous and challenging. We understand each child comes to us with different talents, learning styles, and identities. And we celebrate that difference because our diversity is our strength.

Community is everything at Corlears. It’s who we are and how things get done. Everyone here is motivated by a deep commitment to making sure each child and family feels welcome and included. When everyone feels like they belong, we are stronger and smarter together. We’re a community driven to make the world a better place—starting in our own backyard.

Our Story

Demonstrated by living our core values: compassion, growth, courage to act, responsibility, belonging, curiosity. You can see them cultivated in every experience for students, families and staff.

Community is everything. It’s who we are and how things get done. Everyone here is motivated by a deep commitment to making sure each child and family feels welcome and included. When everyone feels like they belong, we are stronger and smarter together. We’re a community driven to make the world a better place—starting in our own backyard.

BELIEFS THAT UNITE US

Visit Out Sermons

Our community makes us unique. They have an energy that reverberates around them. Their mission in life is to ensure the wonder in the world is not overlooked.

Our Core Values

Learn more about our values and our story as a church by visiting the about us page.

Who are we?

We are welcoming and faith-filled community dedicated to worship, service, and spiritual growth. Our mission is to share God’s love, strengthen our faith, and serve our local community with compassion.

What do we believe?

We believe in the Gospel of Jesus Christ and the power of God’s love to transform lives. Our church upholds the authority of the Bible, the importance of prayer, and the calling to live out our faith through worship, fellowship, and service.

What can I expect when I visit?

You’ll find a warm and friendly atmosphere, uplifting worship, and a message that speaks to your everyday life. Whether you’re new to church or have been attending for years, you are welcome here just as you are.

How can I get involved?

There are many ways to get involved, from joining a small group or Bible study to volunteering in our ministries. Whether you have a heart for serving others, a passion for music, or a desire to grow in faith, we have a place for you!

How can I connect with the church?

We would love to connect with you! You can visit us in person, call us, or email us. You can also follow us on social media and sign up for our newsletter to stay updated on events and services.

Leading with Love

Meet Our Pastors

meet full team

Join us this Sunday

Our community makes us unique. They have an energy that reverberates around them. Their mission in life is to ensure the wonder in the world is not overlooked.

Attend Service in Person

item.title: Author Page
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Home|No breadcrumbs

Anna Rue

The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation.

Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation.

Social Media:
Facebook-f X-twitter Pinterest Instagram

item.title: Your Mission – Should You Choose to Accept It
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words. Their separate existence is a myth.

Why Do We Take on the Impossible?

For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words. Their separate existence is a myth.

What Does Jesus Say About the Impossible?

Believe in Jesus

The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable.

God Loves Us

The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members.

Accepting Your Mission: Impossible

Meeting People Where They Are

Soon after, I found true freedom in Christ. Not only from that season, but from past hurts and burdens as well.

Why Does It Matter?

item.excerpt:encoded: Most of us know exactly where that line comes from. Beginning with the original TV series in 1966 to the current $4B movie franchise starring Tom Cruise, Mission: Impossible has become almost ubiquitous in our pop culture.

item.excerpt:encoded: Sometimes the hardest people group to pray for is family, because there may be a lot of history, years of unresolved issues, or perhaps despite your best efforts, it’s unclear how they will ever make the decision to surrender their lives to Jesus Christ as their Lord and Saviour.

item.title: What’s Your View of Marriage?
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary.

God’s Plan for Marriage is to Reflect His Image

The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental.

The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words. The European languages are members of the same. 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7kI3K3fwg04

Lead Wisely to Maximize Joy

  • Reducing waste is an important part of mindful consumption.
  • Reusing and repurposing items is another way to practice mindful consumption.
  • Recycling is a key component of mindful consumption.
  • Mindful shopping is about making informed choices when it comes to purchasing new items.

Serve Humbly to Multiply Joy

The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words.

What is the Future of Religion

We must choose to obtain, grasp, and receive His peace.  Like salvation, peace is a choice, not a demand. Contact Us

Church Quiz

Please complete all questions. Mark the best answer from the choices given.

[forminator_quiz id="42556"]The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. The European languages are members of the same family.
item.excerpt:encoded: Growing up, I was not enamoured with the thought of my own wedding day. For some odd reasons, I wasn’t consumed looking at wedding magazines nor dreamt about the venue or gown I should have at my future wedding.

","icon_type":"custom","icon":{"value":"far fa-check-circle","library":"fa-regular"},"_id":"60b7ed6"},{"text":"Mindful shopping is about making informed choices when it comes to purchasing new items.","icon_type":"custom","icon":{"value":"far fa-check-circle","library":"fa-regular"},"_id":"3f324fe"}],"__dynamic__":[],"space_between":{"unit":"px","size":"15","sizes":[]},"icon_size":{"unit":"px","size":25,"sizes":[]},"cmsmasters_ribbon_title":"New","__globals__":{"icon_color":"globals/colors?id=accent"},"text_indent":{"unit":"px","size":15,"sizes":[]},"_margin_mobile":{"unit":"px","top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"10","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"cmsmasters-icon-list"},{"id":"8081cd6","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":" Serve Humbly to Multiply Joy ","header_size":"h3","cmsmasters_ribbon_title":"New","typography_font_family":"Varta","typography_font_weight":"700","typography_text_transform":"none","__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"28e295c","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common item.title: How to Develop a Habit of Worship
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul, like these sweet mornings of spring which I enjoy with my whole heart. I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence, that I neglect my talents. I should be incapable of drawing a single stroke at the present moment.
Soon after, I found true freedom in Christ. Not only from that season, but from past hurts and burdens as well.

Explore New Opportunities

A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul, like these sweet mornings of spring which I enjoy with my whole heart. I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence, that I neglect my talents. I should be incapable of drawing a single stroke at the present moment; and yet I feel that I never was a greater artist than now.

Where Does Our Help Come From

I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence, that I neglect my talents. A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul, like these sweet mornings of spring which I enjoy with my whole heart. I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence. A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul. A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul, like these sweet mornings of spring which I enjoy with my whole heart. I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence, that I neglect my talents. I should be incapable of drawing a single stroke at the present moment; and yet I feel that I never was a greater artist than now.

So, Where are We Asking for That Help Today?

I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence, that I neglect my talents. A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul. I should be incapable of drawing a single stroke at the present moment; and yet I feel that I never was a greater artist than now. When, while the lovely valley teems with vapour around me, and the meridian sun strikes the upper surface of the impenetrable foliage of my trees, and but a few stray gleams steal into the inner sanctuary, I throw myself down among the tall grass by the trickling.

Some practical knowledge that I have learned through experience, and my walk with Jesus, is simple. God is a God of peace, not a God of confusion.

John Collins

A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul, like these sweet mornings of spring which I enjoy with my whole heart. I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. I am so happy, my dear friend.

I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence, that I neglect my talents. I should be incapable of drawing a single stroke at the present moment; and yet I feel that I never was a greater artist than now.

Do You Pray for Your Sin?

A wonderful serenity has taken possession of my entire soul, like these sweet mornings of spring which I enjoy with my whole heart. I am alone, and feel the charm of existence in this spot, which was created for the bliss of souls like mine. I am so happy, my dear friend, so absorbed in the exquisite sense of mere tranquil existence.
item.excerpt:encoded: We often talk about developing a daily habit of prayer and reading God’s Word. But what about worship? How do we practically incorporate worship into our busy schedules and develop a habit of worship?

item.title: Experiencing the Freedom of God’s Presence
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words.

How to Help Others

To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words. If several languages coalesce, the grammar of the resulting language is more simple and regular than that of the individual languages. The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be.

We Must Choose to Obtain

How did Religion Evolve?

To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. The European languages are members of the same family.

Our Clients

The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words.

A New Way of Joining Modern Church

To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators.To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words. If several languages coalesce, the grammar of the resulting language is more simple and regular than that of the individual languages. item.title: Discovering the Power of Waiting
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Is it a Good Thing, or a God Thing?

The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. For science, music, sport, etc, Europe uses the same vocabulary. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. To achieve this, it would be necessary to have uniform grammar, pronunciation and more common words. If several languages coalesce, the grammar of the resulting language is more simple and regular than that of the individual languages. The new common language will be more simple and regular than the existing European languages. It will be as simple as Occidental; in fact, it will be Occidental. To an English person, it will seem like simplified English, as a skeptical Cambridge friend of mine told me what Occidental is. The European languages are members of the same family. Their separate existence is a myth. The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators. The European languages are members of the same family.

We are Asking for that Help Today

The languages only differ in their grammar, their pronunciation and their most common words. Everyone realizes why a new common language would be desirable: one could refuse to pay expensive translators.

item.excerpt:encoded: In our day-to-day lives, there are so many things we end up waiting for. We wait for the bus, in traffic, and in the grocery store line. We’ve all sat waiting at the doctor’s office, even the coffee shop.

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Your Community.

Your Church.

Connecting people to God and to each other.

Join Us This Sunday watch live

9:00 and 11:00am

Join Us

Sundays

On demand starting at 5:00 p.m.

You belong here.

Welcome to Faith connect Church

We are a warm and welcoming community of believers committed to growing in faith, serving others, and sharing the love of Christ. Whether you’re exploring faith for the first time or looking for a church to call home, we invite you to join us and experience the love and fellowship of our congregation.

Who we are

A Christ-Centered Community for Worship & Growth

Community Church is an independent, Christ-centered congregation dedicated to worship, spiritual growth, and serving our neighbors. We believe in creating a space where people can authentically encounter God, deepen their faith, and build meaningful relationships.

Our Mission

To share the love of Christ through worship, discipleship, and service.

Our Vision

A thriving community transformed by faith, love, and action.

Our Values

Faith, Community, Service, and Compassion.

You’re always welcome here.

Our Ministries

all ministries

Upcoming Events

Come as you are.

Stay connected with what’s happening at Community Church! From worship nights and Bible studies to outreach programs and fellowship gatherings, there’s always something for everyone.

View All Events

Featured Sermons

Featured Sermons

Don’t miss the next service

Days: Hours: Minutes: Seconds Be the light. Join Us This Sunday

Leading with Love

Meet Our Pastors

meet full team

Support Our Mission

Your generosity helps us serve our community, support missions, and continue sharing the message of hope. Whether you give once or set up recurring donations, every contribution makes a difference.

Get involved today

We’re here for you!

Faith is meant to be lived out in community! Discover ways to connect, serve, and grow with us.

Join a Small Group

Join a Small Group

Build lasting relationships and grow in faith together.

Volunteer

Volunteer

Use your gifts to serve in areas like worship, hospitality, outreach.

Missions & Outreach

Missions & Outreach

Help us make a difference locally and globally.

Seek. Learn. Grow.

Frequently Asked Questions

contact for more

What are your gatherings like?

First, we must understand church is not a building, a Sunday school class, or a service time. According to scripture, a church gathering is a group of people coming together in Christ and the people are the church. Jesus shows us in Matthew 18 that a gathering can be as little as two people together.

What happens when I visit?

The service usually involves the singing of hymns, reading of scripture verses and possibly a psalm. If the church follows a lectionary, the sermon will often be about the scripture lections assigned to that day. Eucharistic churches have usually Holy Communion either every Sunday or several Sundays a month.

What should I bring?

There are no rules as to what to bring to church, other than your self, an open heart and mind. Some bring a bible, if you’re going to engage in bible study.

How should I dress?

If you want to know how to dress for church, something modest and comfortable should be fine. Generally, graphic tees that show off sports team logos or bands should never be worn to church. Solid-colored tops are fine, and even something with a pattern should be OK to wear.

Can I invite people to come with?

Ask your friends if they have a place to worship the Savior and invite them to come worship with you, especially at times like Christmas and Easter. If they aren’t Christians, ask them if they’d like to learn more about Jesus Christ and invite them to experience a Christian worship service with you. Remember to show the same respect for their beliefs that you hope they will give yours.

What if we didn't answer your question?

There are many questions Christians are asked that the individual Christian is unable to answer. It happens all the time.

are your gatherings like?","toggle_content":"<p>First, we must understand church is not a building, a Sunday school class, or a service time. According to scripture, a church gathering is a group of people coming together in Christ and the people are the church. Jesus shows us in Matthew 18 that a gathering can be as little as two people together.<\/p>","_id":"003094a"},{"toggle_title":"What happens when I visit?","toggle_content":"<p>The service usually involves the singing of hymns, reading of scripture verses and possibly a psalm. If the church follows a lectionary, the sermon will often be about the scripture lections assigned to that day. Eucharistic churches have usually Holy Communion either every Sunday or several Sundays a month.<\/p>","_id":"f50b0ae"},{"toggle_title":"What should I bring?","toggle_content":"<p>There are no rules as to what to bring to church, other than your self, an open heart and mind. Your
Community.

Your Church.

Connecting people to God and to each other.

[elementor-element data=""][elementor-element data=""]

9:00 and 11:00am

Join Us

Sundays

On demand starting at 5:00 p.m.

You belong here.

Welcome to Faith connect Church

We are a warm and welcoming community of believers committed to growing in faith, serving others, and sharing the love of Christ. Whether you’re exploring faith for the first time or looking for a church to call home, we invite you to join us and experience the love and fellowship of our congregation.

Who we are

[elementor-element data=""]

Our Mission

To share the love of Christ through worship, discipleship, and service.

Our Vision

A thriving community transformed by faith, love, and action.

Our Values

Faith, Community, Service, and Compassion.

You’re always welcome here.

Our Ministries

[elementor-element data=""]
[elementor-element data=""]

Upcoming Events

Come as you are.

Stay connected with what’s happening at Community Church! From worship nights and Bible studies to outreach programs and fellowship gatherings, there’s always something for everyone.

item.content:encoded:
Sharing Faith, Giving Hope

Join my faith journey!

Ben Shepherd, the founder and lead pastor of Faith Connect Church.

Watch Latest Sermon Join the Community

Welcome Message

Welcome to My Ministry!

I’m Ben Shepherd, and my mission is to share the life-changing power of God’s Word. Whether you’re seeking encouragement, biblical teaching, or a deeper relationship with Christ, you’re in the right place.

Through sermons, devotionals, and outreach, I want to help you grow in faith and experience God’s love in every area of your life.

The Lord is my light and my salvation—whom shall I fear?

– Psalm 27:1

Featured Sermon

Finding Strength in God’s Promises

Watch the latest message on trusting God in uncertain times.

watch now all sermons

Spreading the Word

Sermons Preached

0 +

Podcast Downloads

0 M+

Years in Ministry

0 +

Baptisms Performed

0 +

A Life-Changing book

Beyond Sunday: Living for God every day

A book that dives deep into faith, overcoming struggles and spiritual growth. This inspiring read will challenge, uplift, and strengthen your walk with God.

What You’ll Find Inside:

  • Biblical insights for everyday life
  • Personal stories & testimonies
  • Encouragement for tough seasons
“A powerful journey of faith, wisdom, and God’s grace” Get Your Copy Today!

Sermons & Teachings

Grow in faith through biblical insights and practical teachings.

Explore Sermons

Listen to my podcast

The Faith Connect Podcast

Tune in to The Faith Connect, where Pastor Ben Shepherd shares powerful messages, faith-filled discussions, and practical insights to help you grow spiritually.

New episoders drop every Tuesday on Spotify and Apple Podcasts

Listen Now

Reflections of Faith

Faith & life blog

A blog to inspire, uplift, and strengthen your walk with God.

View All posts

“True wisdom begins with the fear of the Lord; seeking Him brings understanding.”

– Proverbs 9:10

Upcoming events

Join me for worship & teaching

View All eventsNow

Your Questions, Answered

Let’s break it down

What are your gatherings like?

First, we must understand church is not a building, a Sunday school class, or a service time. According to scripture, a church gathering is a group of people coming together in Christ and the people are the church. Jesus shows us in Matthew 18 that a gathering can be as little as two people together.

What happens when I visit?

The service usually involves the singing of hymns, reading of scripture verses and possibly a psalm. If the church follows a lectionary, the sermon will often be about the scripture lections assigned to that day. Eucharistic churches have usually Holy Communion either every Sunday or several Sundays a month.

What should I bring?

There are no rules as to what to bring to church, other than your self, an open heart and mind. Some bring a bible, if you’re going to engage in bible study.

How should I dress?

If you want to know how to dress for church, something modest and comfortable should be fine. Generally, graphic tees that show off sports team logos or bands should never be worn to church. Solid-colored tops are fine, and even something with a pattern should be OK to wear.

Can I invite people to come with?

Ask your friends if they have a place to worship the Savior and invite them to come worship with you, especially at times like Christmas and Easter. If they aren’t Christians, ask them if they’d like to learn more about Jesus Christ and invite them to experience a Christian worship service with you. Remember to show the same respect for their beliefs that you hope they will give yours.

What if we didn't answer your question?

There are many questions Christians are asked that the individual Christian is unable to answer. It happens all the time.

View More FAQs

reach out now

Need prayer or have a question?

down","header_size":"div","_element_width":"auto","_position":"absolute","_offset_orientation_h":"end","_offset_x_end":{"unit":"px","size":29,"sizes":[]},"_offset_x_end_tablet":{"unit":"%","size":8,"sizes":[]},"_offset_x_end_mobile":{"unit":"%","size":"","sizes":[]},"_offset_orientation_v":"end","_z_index":2,"_transform_rotate_popover":"transform","_transform_rotateZ_effect":{"unit":"px","size":-15,"sizes":[]},"cmsmasters_ribbon_title":"New","__globals__":{"typography_typography":"globals\/typography?id=9280a98","title_color":"globals\/colors?id=accent"},"_animation":"zoomIn","hide_mobile":"hidden-mobile"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"}],"isInner":true}],"isInner":true},{"id":"585ee38","elType":"widget","settings":{"toggles":[{"toggle_title":"What are your gatherings like?","toggle_content":"

First, we must understand church is not a building, a Sunday school class, or a service time. According to scripture, a church gathering is a group of people coming together in Christ and the people are the church. Jesus shows us in Matthew 18 that a gathering can be as little as two people together.<\/p>","_id":"003094a"},{"toggle_title":"What happens when I visit?","toggle_content":"

The service usually involves the singing of hymns, reading of scripture verses and possibly a psalm. If the church follows a lectionary, the sermon will often be about the scripture lections assigned to that day. Eucharistic churches have usually Holy Communion either every Sunday or several Sundays a month.<\/p>","_id":"f50b0ae"},{"toggle_title":"What should I bring?","toggle_content":"

There are no rules as to what to bring to church, other than your self, an open heart and mind. Some bring a bible, if you\u2019re going to engage in bible study.<\/p>","_id":"97ae4e9"},{"toggle_title":"How should I dress?","toggle_content":"

If you want to know how to dress for church, something modest and comfortable should be fine. Generally, graphic tees that show off sports team logos or bands should never be worn to church. Solid-colored tops are fine, and even something with a pattern should be OK to wear.<\/p>","_id":"aecca3b"},{"toggle_title":"Can I invite people to come with?","toggle_content":"

Ask your friends if they have a place to worship the Savior and invite them to come worship with you, especially at times like Christmas and Easter. If they aren\u2019t Christians, ask them if they\u2019d like to learn more about Jesus Christ and invite them to experience a Christian worship service with you. Remember to show the same respect for their beliefs that you hope they will give yours.<\/p>","_id":"d41a5dd"},{"toggle_title":"What if we didn't answer your question?","toggle_content":"

There are many questions Christians are asked that the individual Christian is unable to answer. It happens all the time.<\/p>","_id":"25bfaa6"}],"title_html_tag":"h4","trigger_icon":{"value":"cmsmsdemo-icon- cmsms-demo-icon-plus","library":"cmsmasters-demo-icons"},"trigger_active_icon":{"value":"cmsmsdemo-icon- item.title: Home Three
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Worship Anytime, Anywhere

Connecting in faith

Join us live or catch up anytime with past sermons.

Watch live Explore Past Sermons

Next service scheduled in

Days: Hrs: Min: Sec

Wherever you are, you are part of this family. We believe in spreading faith beyond walls, making God’s Word accessible to all.

Join us in worship, stay encouraged, and let’s grow in faith together!

https://youtu.be/CkYUUmuI-Mk Recent live stream

More Live streams

streams archive

Featured Sermons

Serving with love

Encouragement, wisdom, and truth — anytime you need it.

all sermons

Hear Their Stories

Real Stories. Real Faith. Real Impact.

5/5

“During one of the hardest seasons of my life, I found hope through the live streams and prayer support. This ministry has been a blessing!”

Debbie S. 5/5

"I never thought church online could feel this personal, but every message and worship session fills my home with God’s presence."

Sarah W. 5/5

“This church has truly transformed my faith. The sermons speak directly to my heart, and the online community makes me feel connected, even from miles away.”

Ella R. 5/5

“The online Bible studies have deepened my faith like never before. I’ve learned so much and love the connections I’ve made!”

Mia G. 5/5

“This church isn’t just a service—it’s a family. Even though I attend online, I feel more connected than ever.”

Jessica M

Upcoming Events

Come as you are.

Encouragement, wisdom, and truth — anytime you need it.

view all events

Find Your Place

Church is a community to be a part of!

There are so many ways to get connected and make a difference. Whether you’re looking to serve, grow in your faith, or simply meet new people, there’s a place for you here.

Online Bible Studies

God’s Word. Anytime, Anywhere.

Grow in your faith with online Bible studies designed for today’s busy life. Whether you’re new to faith or want to dive deeper, we have study options that fit your schedule.

Give with Purpose

And change lives!

Support our mission and be a part of something bigger.

view Our Campaigns

Experience Worship Wherever You Are!

No matter where life takes you, you can always be a part of something bigger. Join us live for uplifting worship, powerful messages, and a community that welcomes you with open arms.

Join Us This Sunday watch live

Prayer Requests

You’re Not Alone — Let’s Pray Together

We believe in the power of prayer. No matter what you’re facing, we’d love to stand with you and lift up your needs in prayer.

No matter where you are, you are never alone.

Join us in faith, hope, and love.

Hello cmsmasters (not cmsmasters? Log out)

From your account dashboard you can view your recent orders, manage your shipping and billing addresses, and edit your password and account details.

item.title: How Jesus Runs the Church
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded: Far far away, behind the word mountains, far from the countries Vokalia and Consonantia, there live the blind texts. Separated they live in Bookmarksgrove right at the coast of the Semantics, a large language ocean. A small river named Duden flows by their place and supplies it with the necessary regelialia. It is a paradisematic country, in which roasted parts of sentences fly into your mouth. Even the all-powerful Pointing has no control about the blind texts it is an almost unorthographic life.
item.excerpt:encoded: At the heart of Healthy Aging is Dr. Weil’s belief that although aging is an irreversible process, there are myriad things we can do to keep our minds and bodies in good working order through all phases of life.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: This book was beautifully written. Very poetic but still readable.

item.title: Workers for Your Joy
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.excerpt:encoded: At the heart of Healthy Aging is Dr. Weil’s belief that although aging is an irreversible process, there are myriad things we can do to keep our minds and bodies in good working order through all phases of life.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: The book is worth every penny.

item.content:encoded: Far far away, behind the word mountains, far from the countries Vokalia and Consonantia, there live the blind texts. Separated they live in Bookmarksgrove right at the coast of the Semantics, a large language ocean. A small river named Duden flows by their place and supplies it with the necessary regelialia. It is a paradisematic country, in which roasted parts of sentences fly into your mouth. Even the all-powerful Pointing has no control about the blind texts it is an almost unorthographic life.
item.excerpt:encoded: At the heart of Healthy Aging is Dr. Weil’s belief that although aging is an irreversible process, there are myriad things we can do to keep our minds and bodies in good working order through all phases of life.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: I love this book! A must read!

item.title: To Heal And Not To Hurt
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded: Far far away, behind the word mountains, far from the countries Vokalia and Consonantia, there live the blind texts. Separated they live in Bookmarksgrove right at the coast of the Semantics, a large language ocean. A small river named Duden flows by their place and supplies it with the necessary regelialia. It is a paradisematic country, in which roasted parts of sentences fly into your mouth. Even the all-powerful Pointing has no control about the blind texts it is an almost unorthographic life.
item.excerpt:encoded: At the heart of Healthy Aging is Dr. Weil’s belief that although aging is an irreversible process, there are myriad things we can do to keep our minds and bodies in good working order through all phases of life.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content:
This book shows that there is hope for the many many people who suffer from trauma.
And what bigger gift can be given than hope.

item.title: Faithful Leaders
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
The Big Oxmox advised her not to do so, because there were thousands of bad Commas, wild Question Marks and devious Semikoli, but the Little Blind Text didn’t listen. She packed her seven versalia, put her initial into the belt and made herself on the way. When she reached the first hills of the Italic Mountains, she had a last view back on the skyline of her hometown Bookmarksgrove, the headline of Alphabet Village and the subline of her own road, the Line Lane.
Pityful a rethoric question ran over her cheek, then she continued her way. On her way she met a copy. The copy warned the Little Blind Text, that where it came from it would have been rewritten a thousand times and everything that was left from its origin would be the word "and" and the Little Blind Text should turn around and return to its own, safe country.
item.excerpt:encoded: At the heart of Healthy Aging is Dr. Weil’s belief that although aging is an irreversible process, there are myriad things we can do to keep our minds and bodies in good working order through all phases of life.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Everything this man writes is pure gold. He’s a healer and pioneer. His work has fundamentally changed and improved my life.

item.title: Beyond Sunday
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
The Big Oxmox advised her not to do so, because there were thousands of bad Commas, wild Question Marks and devious Semikoli, but the Little Blind Text didn’t listen. She packed her seven versalia, put her initial into the belt and made herself on the way. When she reached the first hills of the Italic Mountains, she had a last view back on the skyline of her hometown Bookmarksgrove, the headline of Alphabet Village and the subline of her own road, the Line Lane.
Pityful a rethoric question ran over her cheek, then she continued her way. On her way she met a copy. The copy warned the Little Blind Text, that where it came from it would have been rewritten a thousand times and everything that was left from its origin would be the word "and" and the Little Blind Text should turn around and return to its own, safe country.
item.excerpt:encoded: At the heart of Healthy Aging is Dr. Weil’s belief that although aging is an irreversible process, there are myriad things we can do to keep our minds and bodies in good working order through all phases of life.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Not an alternative to therapy, but as a first aid this book does its job well.

item.title: The Creation Waits with Eager Longing
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Sermon Notes

Luke 15:11-32 NLT

11 To illustrate the point further, Jesus told them this story: “A man had two sons.
12 The younger son told his father, ‘I want my share of your estate now before you die.’ So his father agreed to divide his wealth between his sons.
13 “A few days later this younger son packed all his belongings and moved to a distant land, and there he wasted all his money in wild living.
14 About the time his money ran out, a great famine swept over the land, and he began to starve.
15 He persuaded a local farmer to hire him, and the man sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.
16 The young man became so hungry that even the pods he was feeding the pigs looked good to him. But no one gave him anything.
17 “When he finally came to his senses, he said to himself, ‘At home even the hired servants have food enough to spare, and here I am dying of hunger!
18 I will go home to my father and say, “Father, I have sinned against both heaven and you,
19 and I am no longer worthy of being called your son. Please take me on as a hired servant.”’
20 “So he returned home to his father. And while he was still a long way off, his father saw him coming. Filled with love and compassion, he ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.
21 His son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against both heaven and you, and I am no longer worthy of being called your son.’
22 “But his father said to the servants, ‘Quick! Bring the finest robe in the house and put it on him. Get a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
23 And kill the calf we have been fattening. We must celebrate with a feast,
24 for this son of mine was dead and has now returned to life. He was lost, but now he is found.’ So the party began.
25 “Meanwhile, the older son was in the fields working. When he returned home, he heard music and dancing in the house,
26 and he asked one of the servants what was going on.
27 ‘Your brother is back,’ he was told, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf. We are celebrating because of his safe return.’
28 “The older brother was angry and wouldn’t go in. His father came out and begged him,
29 but he replied, ‘All these years I’ve slaved for you and never once refused to do a single thing you told me to. And in all that time you never gave me even one young goat for a feast with my friends.
30 Yet when this son of yours comes back after squandering your money on prostitutes, you celebrate by killing the fattened calf!’
31 “His father said to him, ‘Look, dear son, you have always stayed by me, and everything I have is yours.
32 We had to celebrate this happy day. For your brother was dead and has come back to life! He was lost, but now he is found!’”

Share this Sermon:
item.excerpt:encoded: Pastor Greg Laurie shares his testimony and encourages us to give all our worries to God, trusting His plans for us are good.

item.title: Love of God and Love of Neighbour
item.dc:creator: cmsmasters
item.content:encoded:
Sermon Notes

11 To illustrate the point further, Jesus told them this story: “A man had two sons.
12 The younger son told his father, ‘I want my share of your estate now before you die.’ So his father agreed to divide his wealth between his sons.
13 “A few days later this younger son packed all his belongings and moved to a distant land, and there he wasted all his money in wild living.
14 About the time his money ran out, a great famine swept over the land, and he began to starve.
15 He persuaded a local farmer to hire him, and the man sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.

18 I will go home to my father and say, “Father, I have sinned against both heaven and you,

20 “So he returned home to his father. And while he was still a long way off, his father saw him coming. Filled with love and compassion, he ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.
21 His son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against both heaven and you, and I am no longer worthy of being called your son.’
22 “But his father said to the servants, ‘Quick! Bring the finest robe in the house and put it on him. Get a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
23 And kill the calf we have been fattening. We must celebrate with a feast,
24 for this son of mine was dead and has now returned to life. He was lost, but now he is found.’ So the party began.
25 “Meanwhile, the older son was in the fields working. When he returned home, he heard music and dancing in the house,
26 and he asked one of the servants what was going on.
27 ‘Your brother is back,’ he was told, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf. We are celebrating because of his safe return.’
28 “The older brother was angry and wouldn’t go in. His father came out and begged him,
29 but he replied, ‘All these years I’ve slaved for you and never once refused to do a single thing you told me to. And in all that time you never gave me even one young goat for a feast with my friends.
30 Yet when this son of yours comes back after squandering your money on prostitutes, you celebrate by killing the fattened calf!’
31 “His father said to him, ‘Look, dear son, you have always stayed by me, and everything I have is yours.
